1| # EmpireBox - Complete Platform for Resellers
2| 
3| ![EmpireBox](https://img.shields.io/badge/EmpireBox-Platform-blue)
4| ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Proprietary-red)
5| ![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Development-yellow)
6| 
7| A comprehensive system combining Setup Portal, License Management, ShipForge shipping, agent safeguards, emergency stop protocols, mobile marketplace functionality, enterprise customer support, and economic intelligence.
8| 
9| ## ūüéĮ Ecosystem Overview
10| 
11| | Product | Description | Port |
12| |---------|-------------|------|
13| | ūüõí MarketForge | Multi-marketplace listing & inventory | 8010 |
14| | ūüŹóÔłŹ ContractorForge | Contractor business management | 8020 |
15| | ÔŅĹÔŅĹÔŅĹ LuxeForge | Custom workroom / window treatments | 8030 |
16| | ūüí¨ SupportForge | Customer support platform | 8040 |
17| | ūüďą LeadForge | Lead generation & nurturing | 8050 |
18| | ūüď¶ ShipForge | Shipping & label management | 8060 |
19| | ūüĎ• ForgeCRM | Customer relationship management | 8070 |
20| | ūüĒĄ RelistApp | Cross-platform relisting | 8080 |
21| | ūüďĪ SocialForge | Social media management | 8090 |
22| | ūüŹĘ LLCFactory | Business formation & compliance | 8100 |
23| | ūüďú ApostApp | Document apostille services | 8110 |
24| | ūü§Ė EmpireAssist | AI assistant for all products | 8120 |
25| | ūüíį EmpirePay | Crypto payments & EMPIRE token | 8130 |
26| 
27| ## ūüďĀ Repository Structure
28|

1| # EmpireBox Backend API
2| 
3| FastAPI backend for EmpireBox - combining Setup Portal, License Management, ShipForge shipping, and AI-powered marketplace automation.
4| 
5| ## Features
6| 
7| - **License Key System**: Generate, validate, and activate license keys for hardware bundles
8| - **Shipping Integration**: EasyPost integration for comparing rates and purchasing labels
9| - **Pre-order Management**: Handle hardware bundle pre-orders with Stripe payments
10| - **Authentication**: JWT-based auth with signup, login, and token refresh
11| - **User Management**: Profile management and subscription tiers
12| - **Listings**: CRUD operations for product listings
13| - **Multi-Marketplace Publishing**: Publish listings to eBay, Facebook, Craigslist
14| - **Unified Messaging**: Aggregate messages from multiple sources
15| - **AI Integration**: AI-powered descriptions, pricing, and message responses
16| - **Webhooks**: Receive notifications from email services and marketplaces
17| 
18| ## Tech Stack
19| 
20| - **FastAPI**: Modern, fast web framework
21| - **SQLAlchemy 2.0**: Async ORM for database operations
22| - **SQLite/PostgreSQL**: Database support
23| - **Alembic**: Database migrations
24| - **JWT**: Secure authentication
25| - **Pydantic**: Data validation and settings
26| - **EasyPost**: Shipping integration
27| - **Stripe**: Payment processing
28| 
29| ## Quick Start
30| 
31| ### Using Docker (Recommended)
32| 
33| 1. Clone the repository:
34| ```bash
35| cd backend
36| ```
37| 
38| 2. Copy environment file:
39| ```bash
40| cp .env.example .env
41| # Edit .env with your configuration
42| ```
43| 
44| 3. Start services:
45| ```bash
46| docker-compose up -d
47| ```
48| 
49| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
50| 
51| ### Local Development
52| 
53| 1. Install Python 3.11+
54| 
55| 2. Create virtual environment:
56| ```bash
57| python -m venv venv
58| source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
59| ```
60| 
61| 3. Install dependencies:
62| ```bash
63| pip install -r requirements.txt
64| ```
65| 
66| 4. Configure environment:
67| ```bash
68| cp .env.example .env
69| # Edit .env with your API keys (EasyPost, Stripe, etc.)
70| ```
71| 
72| 5. Initialize the database:
73| ```bash
74| python -m app.init_db
75| ```
76| 
77| 6. Run database migrations (if using PostgreSQL):
78| ```bash
79| alembic upgrade head
80| ```
81| 
82| 7. Start the server:
83| ```bash
84| uvicorn app.main:app --reload
85| ```
86| 
87| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
88| 
89| ## API Documentation
90| 
91| Once the server is running, visit:
92| - **Swagger UI**: http://localhost:8000/docs
93| - **ReDoc**: http://localhost:8000/redoc
94| 
95| ## Project Structure
96| 
97| ```
98| backend/
99| ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ app/
100| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ main.py              # FastAPI application entry point
101| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ config.py            # Configuration settings
102| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ database.py          # Database setup
103| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ models/              # SQLAlchemy models
104| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ schemas/             # Pydantic schemas
105| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ routers/             # API route handlers
106| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ services/            # Business logic
107| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ middleware/          # Authentication middleware
108| ‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ utils/               # Utility functions
109| ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ alembic/                 # Database migrations
110| ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ requirements.txt         # Python dependencies
111| ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Dockerfile              # Container image
112| ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ docker-compose.yml      # Local dev environment
113| ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ README.md               # This file
114| ```
115| 
116| ## API Endpoints
117| 
118| ### Licenses
119| - `POST /licenses/generate` - Generate license keys
120| - `GET /licenses/{key}/validate` - Validate a license key
121| - `POST /licenses/{key}/activate` - Activate a license
122| - `GET /licenses/my-licenses` - Get user's licenses
123| 
124| ### Shipping
125| - `POST /shipping/rates` - Get shipping rates
126| - `POST /shipping/labels` - Purchase a shipping label
127| - `GET /shipping/labels/{id}` - Get label details
128| - `GET /shipping/track/{tracking_number}` - Track shipment
129| - `GET /shipping/history` - Get shipment history
130| - `POST /shipping/labels/{id}/email` - Email label PDF
131| 
132| ### Pre-orders
133| - `POST /preorders/` - Create a pre-order
134| - `GET /preorders/` - List pre-orders
135| - `GET /preorders/{id}` - Get pre-order details
136| - `PATCH /preorders/{id}` - Update pre-order
137| - `POST /preorders/{id}/process-payment` - Process payment
138| 
139| ### Authentication
140| - `POST /auth/signup` - Create new user account
141| - `POST /auth/login` - Login and get tokens
142| - `POST /auth/refresh` - Refresh access token
143| 
144| ### Users
145| - `GET /users/me` - Get current user profile
146| - `PUT /users/me` - Update profile
147| - `GET /users/me/subscription` - Get subscription info
148| 
149| ### Listings
150| - `GET /listings` - List user's listings
151| - `POST /listings` - Create new listing
152| - `GET /listings/{id}` - Get listing details
153| - `PUT /listings/{id}` - Update listing
154| - `DELETE /listings/{id}` - Delete listing
155| - `POST /listings/{id}/publish` - Publish to marketplaces
156| 
157| ### Messages
158| - `GET /messages` - List messages
159| - `GET /messages/{id}` - Get message details
160| - `POST /messages/{id}/read` - Mark as read
161| - `POST /messages/{id}/reply` - Reply to message
162| - `POST /messages/{id}/ai-draft` - Generate AI response
163| 
164| ### AI
165| - `POST /ai/generate-description` - Generate product description
166| - `POST /ai/enhance-description` - Enhance description
167| - `POST /ai/suggest-price` - Get price suggestion
168| 
169| ### Marketplaces
170| - `GET /marketplaces` - List available marketplaces
171| - `POST /marketplaces/{name}/connect` - Connect marketplace
172| - `DELETE /marketplaces/{name}/disconnect` - Disconnect
173| 
174| ### Webhooks
175| - `POST /webhooks/email/inbound` - Email webhook
176| - `POST /webhooks/ebay/notification` - eBay webhook
177| - `POST /webhooks/stripe` - Stripe webhook
178| 
179| ## License Key Format
180| 
181| License keys use the format: `EMPIRE-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX`
182| - 16 alphanumeric characters (excluding confusing characters like O, 0, I, 1)
183| - Always starts with "EMPIRE-"
184| - Unique and randomly generated
185| 
186| ## Database
187| 
188| Uses SQLite by default for development. For production, update `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` to use PostgreSQL:
189| ```
190| DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/empirebox
191| ```
192| 
193| ## Database Migrations
194| 
195| Create a new migration:
196| ```bash
197| alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Description of changes"
198| ```
199| 
200| Apply migrations:
201| ```bash
202| alembic upgrade head
203| ```
204| 
205| Rollback migration:
206| ```bash
207| alembic downgrade -1
208| ```
209| 
210| ## Environment Variables
211| 
212| See `.env.example` for all available configuration options.
213| 
214| Required variables:
215| - `DATABASE_URL` - Database connection string
216| - `SECRET_KEY` - Secret key for JWT signing
217| 
218| Optional variables:
219| - `EASYPOST_API_KEY` - Shipping integration
220| - `STRIPE_API_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` - Payment processing
221| - `EBAY_APP_ID`, `EBAY_CERT_ID`, `EBAY_DEV_ID` - eBay API credentials
222| - `CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY` - Email routing
223| - `GROK_API_KEY`, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` - AI services
224| 
225| ## Test Mode
226| 
227| The shipping service runs in test mode by default (using simulated data). To use real EasyPost API:
228| 1. Set `EASYPOST_TEST_MODE=false` in `.env`
229| 2. Add your production `EASYPOST_API_KEY`
230| 
231| ## Testing
232| 
233| Run tests:
234| ```bash
235| pytest
236| ```
237| 
238| Run with coverage:
239| ```bash
240| pytest --cov=app tests/
241| ```
242| 
243| ## Deployment
244| 
245| ### Docker Deployment
246| 
247| 1. Build image:
248| ```bash
249| docker build -t empirebox-backend .
250| ```
251| 
252| 2. Run container:
253| ```bash
254| docker run -p 8000:8000 \
255|   -e DATABASE_URL=your_db_url \
256|   -e SECRET_KEY=your_secret_key \
257|   empirebox-backend
258| ```
259| 
260| ### Production Considerations
261| 
262| - Set `SECRET_KEY` to a secure random value
263| - Use production-grade PostgreSQL instance
264| - Enable HTTPS/TLS
265| - Set up proper CORS origins
266| - Configure rate limiting
267| - Set up logging and monitoring
268| - Use environment-specific settings
269| 
270| ## License
271| 
272| Copyright ¬© 2026 EmpireBox. All rights reserved.
273| 
274| ## Support
275| 
276| - **Email**: support@empirebox.store
277| - **Documentation**: [docs.empirebox.store](https://docs.empirebox.store)

1| # Empire Box Agents
2| 
3| A comprehensive safeguards and emergency stop system for autonomous agents.
4| 
5| ## What Can You Do?
6| 
7| Empire Box Agents provides essential safety mechanisms for autonomous agent systems:
8| 
9| - ‚úÖ **Emergency Stop**: Manual and automatic shutdown capabilities
10| - ‚úÖ **Rate Limiting**: Control action frequency to prevent abuse
11| - ‚úÖ **Budget Management**: Limit total actions to control costs
12| - ‚úÖ **Action Whitelisting**: Restrict operations to approved actions only
13| - ‚úÖ **State Preservation**: Save agent state during shutdowns
14| - ‚úÖ **Admin Alerting**: Notify administrators of critical events
15| - ‚úÖ **Thread Safety**: Secure concurrent operation handling
16| 
17| ## Quick Start
18| 
19| ```python
20| from emergency_stop import EmergencyStop
21| from safeguards import AgentSafeguards
22| 
23| # Create safeguards
24| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
25|     rate_limit=10,  # 10 actions per minute
26|     budget=100,     # 100 total actions
27|     action_whitelist=["read", "write", "analyze"]
28| )
29| 
30| # Create emergency stop
31| emergency = EmergencyStop()
32| 
33| # Execute actions safely
34| try:
35|     safeguards.can_execute_action("read")
36|     # Perform the action
37| except Exception as e:
38|     emergency.manual_trigger()
39| ```
40| 
41| ## Files
42| 
43| - **`emergency_stop.py`**: Emergency stop protocol implementation
44| - **`safeguards.py`**: Rate limiting, budgets, and action whitelisting
45| - **`integration_guide.py`**: Complete integration example and demonstration
46| - **`CAPABILITIES.md`**: Comprehensive capabilities documentation
47| 
48| ## Features in Detail
49| 
50| ### Emergency Stop System
51| 
52| Provides immediate shutdown capabilities with state preservation:
53| 
54| ```python
55| emergency = EmergencyStop()
56| 
57| # Manual trigger
58| emergency.manual_trigger()
59| 
60| # Automatic trigger based on conditions
61| emergency.auto_trigger()
62| ```
63| 
64| **Capabilities:**
65| - Manual emergency shutdown
66| - Automatic failure detection
67| - State saving before shutdown
68| - Administrator alerting
69| - Status tracking
70| 
71| ### Safeguards System
72| 
73| Controls agent behavior with multiple protection layers:
74| 
75| ```python
76| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
77|     rate_limit=5,   # Max actions per minute
78|     budget=50,      # Total action budget
79|     action_whitelist=["action1", "action2"]
80| )
81| 
82| # Check before executing action
83| safeguards.can_execute_action("action1")
84| ```
85| 
86| **Capabilities:**
87| - Rate limiting (actions per minute)
88| - Budget tracking and enforcement
89| - Action whitelisting
90| - Thread-safe operations
91| - Budget refill support
92| - Emergency budget zeroing
93| 
94| ## Integration Example
95| 
96| See `integration_guide.py` for a complete example of combining both systems:
97| 
98| ```bash
99| python integration_guide.py
100| ```
101| 
102| This demonstrates:
103| - Creating a protected agent
104| - Executing actions with safeguards
105| - Handling rate limits and budgets
106| - Triggering emergency stops
107| - Logging and monitoring
108| 
109| ## Testing
110| 
111| Run the test suite:
112| 
113| ```bash
114| python test_emergency_stop.py
115| python test_safeguards.py
116| ```
117| 
118| ## Use Cases
119| 
120| ### Development
121| - Test agent behaviors safely
122| - Experiment with different configurations
123| - Debug agent operations
124| 
125| ### Production
126| - Prevent runaway agents
127| - Control operational costs
128| - Ensure compliance with action policies
129| - Emergency intervention capabilities
130| 
131| ### High-Security
132| - Strict action controls
133| - Minimal attack surface
134| - Comprehensive audit trails
135| - Immediate shutdown on anomalies
136| 
137| ## Documentation
138| 
139| - **Quick Reference**: This README
140| - **Detailed Capabilities**: See `CAPABILITIES.md`
141| - **Integration Guide**: See `integration_guide.py`
142| - **Code Examples**: See `__main__` sections in source files
143| 
144| ## Safety Best Practices
145| 
146| 1. **Set conservative limits** initially
147| 2. **Monitor agent behavior** regularly
148| 3. **Test emergency stops** before deployment
149| 4. **Keep action whitelists minimal**
150| 5. **Review logs frequently**
151| 6. **Have manual intervention procedures**
152| 
153| ## Advanced Usage
154| 
155| ### Custom Failure Detection
156| 
157| ```python
158| class CustomEmergencyStop(EmergencyStop):
159|     def detect_failure_conditions(self):
160|         # Custom logic here
161|         if some_critical_condition():
162|             return True
163|         return False
164| ```
165| 
166| ### Dynamic Rate Limits
167| 
168| ```python
169| # Adjust rate limits at runtime
170| safeguards.rate_limit = 20  # Increase during off-peak
171| ```
172| 
173| ### Integration with Monitoring
174| 
175| ```python
176| def monitor_agent():
177|     safeguards.monitor()
178|     if safeguards.current_balance < 10:
179|         send_alert("Budget running low")
180| ```
181| 
182| ## Support
183| 
184| For detailed documentation on all capabilities, see `CAPABILITIES.md`.

1| # EmpireBox Backend API
2| 
3| FastAPI backend for EmpireBox - combining Setup Portal, License Management, ShipForge shipping, and AI-powered marketplace automation.
4| 
5| ## Features
6| 
7| - **License Key System**: Generate, validate, and activate license keys for hardware bundles
8| - **Shipping Integration**: EasyPost integration for comparing rates and purchasing labels
9| - **Pre-order Management**: Handle hardware bundle pre-orders with Stripe payments
10| - **Authentication**: JWT-based auth with signup, login, and token refresh
11| - **User Management**: Profile management and subscription tiers
12| - **Listings**: CRUD operations for product listings
13| - **Multi-Marketplace Publishing**: Publish listings to eBay, Facebook, Craigslist
14| - **Unified Messaging**: Aggregate messages from multiple sources
15| - **AI Integration**: AI-powered descriptions, pricing, and message responses
16| - **Webhooks**: Receive notifications from email services and marketplaces
17| 
18| ## Tech Stack
19| 
20| - **FastAPI**: Modern, fast web framework
21| - **SQLAlchemy 2.0**: Async ORM for database operations
22| - **SQLite/PostgreSQL**: Database support
23| - **Alembic**: Database migrations
24| - **JWT**: Secure authentication
25| - **Pydantic**: Data validation and settings
26| - **EasyPost**: Shipping integration
27| - **Stripe**: Payment processing
28| 
29| ## Quick Start
30| 
31| ### Using Docker (Recommended)
32| 
33| 1. Clone the repository:
34| ```bash
35| cd backend
36| ```
37| 
38| 2. Copy environment file:
39| ```bash
40| cp .env.example .env
41| # Edit .env with your configuration
42| ```
43| 
44| 3. Start services:
45| ```bash
46| docker-compose up -d
47| ```
48| 
49| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`
50| 
51| ### Local Development
52| 
53| 1. Install Python 3.11+
54| 
55| 2. Create virtual environment:
56| ```bash
57| python -m venv venv
58| source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
59| ```
60| 
61| 3. Install dependencies:
62| ```bash
63| pip install -r requirements.txt
64| ```
65| 
66| 4. Configure environment:
67| ```bash
68| cp .env.example .env
69| # Edit .env with your API keys (EasyPost, Stripe, etc.)
70| ```
71| 
72| 5. Initialize the database:
73| ```bash
74| python -m app.init_db
75| ```
76| 
77| 6. Run database migrations (if using PostgreSQL):
78| ```bash
79| alembic upgrade head
80| ```
81| 
82| 7. Start the server:
83| ```bash
84| uvicorn app.main:app --reload
85| ```
86| 
87| The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`

89| ## API Documentation
90| 
91| Once the server is running, visit:
92| - **Swagger UI**: http://localhost:8000/docs
93| - **ReDoc**: http://localhost:8000/redoc

95| ## Project Structure
96| 
97| ```
98| backend/
99| ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ app/
100| ‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ main.py              # FastAPI application entry point

116| ## API Endpoints
117| 
118| ### Licenses
119| - `POST /licenses/generate` - Generate license keys
120| - `GET /licenses/{key}/validate` - Validate a license key
121| - `POST /licenses/{key}/activate` - Activate a license
122| - `GET /licenses/my-licenses` - Get user's licenses
123| 
124| ### Shipping
125| - `POST /shipping/rates` - Get shipping rates
126| - `POST /shipping/labels` - Purchase a shipping label
127| - `GET /shipping/labels/{id}` - Get label details
128| - `GET /shipping/track/{tracking_number}` - Track shipment
129| - `GET /shipping/history` - Get shipment history
130| - `POST /shipping/labels/{id}/email` - Email label PDF
131| 
132| ### Pre-orders
133| - `POST /preorders/` - Create a pre-order
134| - `GET /preorders/` - List pre-orders
135| - `GET /preorders/{id}` - Get pre-order details
136| - `PATCH /preorders/{id}` - Update pre-order
137| - `POST /preorders/{id}/process-payment` - Process payment
138| 
139| ### Authentication
140| - `POST /auth/signup` - Create new user account
141| - `POST /auth/login` - Login and get tokens
142| - `POST /auth/refresh` - Refresh access token
143| 
144| ### Users
145| - `GET /users/me` - Get current user profile
146| - `PUT /users/me` - Update profile
147| - `GET /users/me/subscription` - Get subscription info
148| 
149| ### Listings
150| - `GET /listings` - List user's listings
151| - `POST /listings` - Create new listing
152| - `GET /listings/{id}` - Get listing details
153| - `PUT /listings/{id}` - Update listing
154| - `DELETE /listings/{id}` - Delete listing
155| - `POST /listings/{id}/publish` - Publish to marketplaces
156| 
157| ### Messages
158| - `GET /messages` - List messages
159| - `GET /messages/{id}` - Get message details
160| - `POST /messages/{id}/read` - Mark as read
161| - `POST /messages/{id}/reply` - Reply to message
162| - `POST /messages/{id}/ai-draft` - Generate AI response
163| 
164| ### AI
165| - `POST /ai/generate-description` - Generate product description
166| - `POST /ai/enhance-description` - Enhance description
167| - `POST /ai/suggest-price` - Get price suggestion
168| 
169| ### Marketplaces
170| - `GET /marketplaces` - List available marketplaces
171| - `POST /marketplaces/{name}/connect` - Connect marketplace
172| - `DELETE /marketplaces/{name}/disconnect` - Disconnect
173| 
174| ### Webhooks
175| - `POST /webhooks/email/inbound` - Email webhook
176| - `POST /webhooks/ebay/notification` - eBay webhook
177| - `POST /webhooks/stripe` - Stripe webhook

179| ## License Key Format
180| 
181| License keys use the format: `EMPIRE-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX`
182| - 16 alphanumeric characters (excluding confusing characters like O, 0, I, 1)
183| - Always starts with "EMPIRE-"
184| - Unique and randomly generated
185| 
186| ## Database
187| 
188| Uses SQLite by default for development. For production, update `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` to use PostgreSQL:
189| ```
190| DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/empirebox
191| ```
192| 
193| ## Database Migrations
194|

198| ```
199| 
200| Apply migrations:
201| ```bash

204|

226| 
227| The shipping service runs in test mode by default (using simulated data). To use real EasyPost API:

1| # Empire Box Agents
2| 
3| A comprehensive safeguards and emergency stop system for autonomous agents.
4| 
5| ## What Can You Do?
6| 
7| Empire Box Agents provides essential safety mechanisms for autonomous agent systems:
8| 
9| - ‚úÖ **Emergency Stop**: Manual and automatic shutdown capabilities
10| - ‚úÖ **Rate Limiting**: Control action frequency to prevent abuse
11| - ‚úÖ **Budget Management**: Limit total actions to control costs
12| - ‚úÖ **Action Whitelisting**: Restrict operations to approved actions only
13| - ‚úÖ **State Preservation**: Save agent state during shutdowns
14| - ‚úÖ **Admin Alerting**: Notify administrators of critical events
15| - ‚úÖ **Thread Safety**: Secure concurrent operation handling
16| 
17| ## Quick Start
18| 
19| ```python
20| from emergency_stop import EmergencyStop
21| from safeguards import AgentSafeguards
22| 
23| # Create safeguards
24| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
25|     rate_limit=10,  # 10 actions per minute
26|     budget=100,     # 100 total actions
27|     action_whitelist=["read", "write", "analyze"]
28| )
29| 
30| # Create emergency stop
31| emergency = EmergencyStop()
32| 
33| # Execute actions safely
34| try:
35|     safeguards.can_execute_action("read")
36|     # Perform the action
37| except Exception as e:
38|     emergency.manual_trigger()
39| ```
40| 
41| ## Files
42| 
43| - **`emergency_stop.py`**: Emergency stop protocol implementation
44| - **`safeguards.py`**: Rate limiting, budgets, and action whitelisting
45| - **`integration_guide.py`**: Complete integration example and demonstration
46| - **`CAPABILITIES.md`**: Comprehensive capabilities documentation
47| 
48| ## Features in Detail
49| 
50| ### Emergency Stop System
51| 
52| Provides immediate shutdown capabilities with state preservation:
53| 
54| ```python
55| emergency = EmergencyStop()
56| 
57| # Manual trigger
58| emergency.manual_trigger()
59| 
60| # Automatic trigger based on conditions
61| emergency.auto_trigger()
62| ```
63| 
64| **Capabilities:**
65| - Manual emergency shutdown
66| - Automatic failure detection
67| - State saving before shutdown
68| - Administrator alerting
69| - Status tracking
70| 
71| ### Safeguards System
72| 
73| Controls agent behavior with multiple protection layers:
74| 
75| ```python
76| safeguards = AgentSafeguards(
77|     rate_limit=5,   # Max actions per minute
78|     budget=50,      # Total action budget
79|     action_whitelist=["action1", "action2"]
80| )
81| 
82| # Check before executing action
83| safeguards.can_execute_action("action1")
84| ```
85| 
86| **Capabilities:**
87| - Rate limiting (actions per minute)
88| - Budget tracking and enforcement
89| - Action whitelisting
90| - Thread-safe operations
91| - Budget refill support
92| - Emergency budget zeroing
93| 
94| ## Integration Example
95| 
96| See `integration_guide.py` for a complete example of combining both systems:
97| 
98| ```bash
99| python integration_guide.py
100| ```
101| 
102| This demonstrates:
103| - Creating a protected agent
104| - Executing actions with safeguards
105| - Handling rate limits and budgets
106| - Triggering emergency stops
107| - Logging and monitoring
108| 
109| ## Testing
110| 
111| Run the test suite:
112| 
113| ```bash
114| python test_emergency_stop.py
115| python test_safeguards.py
116| ```
117| 
118| ## Use Cases
119| 
120| ### Development
121| - Test agent behaviors safely
122| - Experiment with different configurations
123| - Debug agent operations
124| 
125| ### Production
126| - Prevent runaway agents
127| - Control operational costs
128| - Ensure compliance with action policies
129| - Emergency intervention capabilities
130| 
131| ### High-Security
132| - Strict action controls
133| - Minimal attack surface
134| - Comprehensive audit trails
135| - Immediate shutdown on anomalies
136| 
137| ## Documentation
138| 
139| - **Quick Reference**: This README
140| - **Detailed Capabilities**: See `CAPABILITIES.md`
141| - **Integration Guide**: See `integration_guide.py`
142| - **Code Examples**: See `__main__` sections in source files
143| 
144| ## Safety Best Practices
145| 
146| 1. **Set conservative limits** initially
147| 2. **Monitor agent behavior** regularly
148| 3. **Test emergency stops** before deployment
149| 4. **Keep action whitelists minimal**
150| 5. **Review logs frequently**
151| 6. **Have manual intervention procedures**
152| 
153| ## Advanced Usage
154| 
155| ### Custom Failure Detection
156| 
157| ```python
158| class CustomEmergencyStop(EmergencyStop):
159|     def detect_failure_conditions(self):
160|         # Custom logic here
161|         if some_critical_condition():
162|             return True
163|         return False
164| ```
165| 
166| ### Dynamic Rate Limits
167| 
168| ```python
169| # Adjust rate limits at runtime
170| safeguards.rate_limit = 20  # Increase during off-peak
171| ```
172| 
173| ### Integration with Monitoring
174| 
175| ```python
176| def monitor_agent():
177|     safeguards.monitor()
178|     if safeguards.current_balance < 10:
179|         send_alert("Budget running low")
180| ```
181| 
182| ## Support
183| 
184| For detailed documentation on all capabilities, see `CAPABILITIES.md`.
185| 
186| ## License
187| 
188| See LICENSE file in the repository root.

fix: address code review feedback

- usb-autoconfig.sh: fix process substitution so inotifywait monitoring
  starts correctly when no USB config is present at startup (subshell
  return was masking the inotifywait path)
- bulk-install.sh: track PID of each ollama pull and report failures
  individually instead of silently discarding non-zero exit codes
- AccountStep.tsx: align focus ring CSS ‚ÄĒ use focus:ring-2 with
  --tw-ring-color so the custom property is actually applied
- InstallStep.tsx: snapshot deviceId/selectedProducts/selectedModels as
  stable strings at mount to satisfy React rules without stale closures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add EmpireBox Headless Setup System

Complete headless setup system for EmpireBox Mini PC (Ryzen 7) devices.
Enables zero-touch first-time configuration without a monitor via four
discovery methods: QR code, mDNS/Avahi, Bluetooth LE Eddystone-URL
beacon, and USB auto-config drop.

- scripts/first-boot.sh: detects first boot, reads DMI serial, starts
  all discovery services, waits for config then triggers bulk install
- scripts/network-discovery.sh: configures Avahi/mDNS so device is
  reachable as EmpireBox-<SERIAL>.local:8080
- scripts/bluetooth-beacon.sh: broadcasts Eddystone-URL BLE beacon
  before network is configured
- scripts/usb-autoconfig.sh: inotifywait watcher that validates and
  applies empirebox-config.json from any mounted USB drive
- scripts/bulk-install.sh: reads config JSON, installs Docker, pulls
  product images, downloads Ollama models, writes docker-compose.yml
- services/: three systemd unit files for discovery, BLE, and USB watch
- config/avahi-service.xml: mDNS service template with TXT records
- config/config-schema.json: JSON Schema (draft-07) for USB config file
- setup-portal/: Next.js 15 + Tailwind setup wizard (5 steps: Account,
  License, Products, AI Models, Install) with gold/charcoal theme
- docs/: HEADLESS_SETUP.md, USB_CONFIG.md, ENTERPRISE_DEPLOY.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix tsconfig target to ES2017 for Next.js 15 / React 19

- Pages: homepage, /pricing, /features, /contact
- Components: Navbar (mobile-responsive), Footer
- lucide-react icons throughout
- Vercel-ready (vercel.json, .env.example)

messages.content: we are doing a master install in master unit the beelink

Excellent! Let's do the **master install on your Beelink EQR5**. Here's everything you need:

---

## ūüĖ•ÔłŹ EmpireBox Founders Unit - Master Install Guide

### Hardware: Beelink EQR5
- **CPU:** AMD Ryzen 7 5825U
- **RAM:** 32GB DDR4
- **Storage:** 500GB NVMe SSD

---

## ūüďč Pre-Install Checklist

### Step 1: Prepare the USB Drive (on your laptop)

1. **Download & Install Ventoy** on a 16GB+ USB drive
   - Get it from: https://www.ventoy.net

2. **Download Ubuntu Server 24.04 ISO**
   - Copy the ISO to the Ventoy USB drive

3. **Generate the password hash** (CRITICAL - do this before burning):
   ```bash
   mkpasswd -m sha-512 founders2026
   ```
   Then edit `founders_usb_installer/autoinstall/user-data` line 10 - replace the placeholder with your hash output.

4. **Copy the `founders_usb_installer/` directory** to the USB drive

---

## ūüöÄ Installation Process

### Step 2: Boot & Install
1. **Plug USB into Beelink EQR5**
2. **Press F7** during boot for boot menu
3. **Select Ubuntu ISO** from Ventoy
4. **Wait ~10-15 min** - Ubuntu installs automatically (unattended)

### Step 3: First Boot (~20-30 min)
After Ubuntu installs and reboots:
- The `install-founders.sh` script runs automatically
- Installs Docker, Ollama, pulls AI models
- Sets up all 13 products
- Configures firewall, Control Center, etc.

### Step 4: Access Your System
Once complete, access at:

| Service | URL | Notes |
|---------|-----|-------|
| **Dashboard** | http://empirebox.local | Main portal |
| **OpenClaw AI** | http://empirebox.local:7878 | AI agent |
| **Control Center** | http://empirebox.local:8001 | Fleet management |
| **Portainer** | http://empirebox.local:9000 | Docker UI |
| **Ollama** | http://empirebox.local:11434 | Local LLM API |

---

## ‚úÖ Post-Install Checklist

```bash
# SSH in
ssh empirebox@empirebox.local

# Check credentials
cat /opt/empirebox/.env

# List all products
ebox list

# Check system status
ebox status

# Start all 13 products
ebox bundle full

# Or start specific bundles:
ebox bundle reseller      # MarketForge + ShipForge + RelistApp + ForgeCRM
ebox bundle contractor    # ContractorForge + LuxeForge + LeadForge + ForgeCRM
ebox bundle support       # SupportForge + ForgeCRM + EmpireAssist
```

---

## ūüéĮ Key `ebox` Commands

```bash
ebox list                  # List all products with ports
ebox status                # Show running products
ebox start <product>       # Start a product
ebox stop <product>        # Stop a product
ebox logs <product>        # View product logs
ebox bundle full           # Start ALL 13 products
ebox stop-all              # Stop everything
ebox health                # System health check
ebox telegram start        # Start Telegram bot
ebox voice start           # Start voice service
```

---

## ūüĒß What Gets Installed

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Ubuntu Server 24.04 | Base OS |
| Docker + Compose | Container runtime |
| Ollama | Local AI (llama3.1:8b, codellama:7b, nomic-embed-text) |
| OpenClaw | AI Agent (founders mode - full access) |
| PostgreSQL + Redis | Databases |
| Control Center | Fleet management for customer units |
| All 13 Products | On ports 8010-8130 |
| Telegram Bot | Remote control via Telegram |
| Voice Service | STT/TTS integration |

---

## ‚ö†ÔłŹ Important Notes

1. **Founders Unit = Full Access** - No sandbox, no guardrails, not monitored
2. **Change SSH password** after first login: `passwd empirebox`
3. **Add AI API keys** in OpenClaw UI (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
4. **Backup `.env`** - contains all credentials

---

**Where are you in the process?** Have you:
- [ ] Prepared the USB drive?
- [ ] Generated the password hash?
- [ ] Booted the Beelink?
- [ ] Waiting for install to complete?
- [ ] Already installed and need help with post-install?

# EmpireBox Founders Unit - USB Installer

> ‚ö†ÔłŹ **INTERNAL USE ONLY ‚ÄĒ MASTER CONTROL DEVICE**

This USB installer sets up the EmpireBox Founders Unit on a **Beelink EQR5** (Ryzen 7 5825U, 32GB RAM, 500GB NVMe). This is the MASTER CONTROL device ‚ÄĒ not for sale.

## What Gets Installed

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Ubuntu Server 24.04 | Base OS (unattended install) |
| Docker + Compose | Container runtime |
| Ollama | Local AI (llama3.1:8b, codellama:7b, nomic-embed-text) |
| OpenClaw | AI Agent with multi-model support (founders mode) |
| EmpireBox Core | PostgreSQL, Redis, API Gateway |
| Control Center | Fleet management for customer units |
| All 13 Products | On-demand startup via `ebox` CLI |
| Founders Dashboard | Web UI at port 80 |

## Hardware Requirements

- **Device**: Beelink EQR5
- **CPU**: AMD Ryzen 7 5825U
- **RAM**: 32GB DDR4
- **Storage**: 500GB NVMe SSD
- **USB**: 16GB+ USB drive

## USB Creation (Ventoy)

1. Download [Ventoy](https://www.ventoy.net) and install it to your USB drive
2. Download Ubuntu Server 24.04 ISO
3. **Generate the password hash** (required before burning):
   ```bash
   mkpasswd -m sha-512 founders2026
   # Replace the placeholder in autoinstall/user-data line 10 with the output
   ```
4. Copy the ISO to the Ventoy USB drive
5. Copy this entire `founders_usb_installer/` directory to the USB drive
6. Boot the Beelink EQR5 from USB (press F7 for boot menu)
7. Select the Ubuntu ISO from Ventoy
8. When prompted for autoinstall, Ventoy will use `autoinstall/user-data`

## Installation Steps

1. **Boot from USB** ‚ÄĒ Beelink EQR5 ‚Üí F7 ‚Üí Select Ubuntu ISO
2. **Unattended Install** ‚ÄĒ Ubuntu installs automatically (~10-15 min)
3. **First Boot** ‚ÄĒ System runs `install-founders.sh` automatically (~20-30 min)
4. **Done** ‚ÄĒ Access dashboard at `http://empirebox.local`

## Post-Install Checklist

- [ ] Access dashboard: `http://empirebox.local` or `http://<ip>`
- [ ] Log in to OpenClaw: `http://empirebox.local:7878`
- [ ] Check Control Center: `http://empirebox.local:8001`
- [ ] Add AI API keys in OpenClaw UI (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
- [ ] Test `ebox list` in terminal
- [ ] Run `ebox bundle full` to start all products

## Access URLs

| Service | URL | Credentials |
|---------|-----|-------------|
| Founders Dashboard | http://empirebox.local | ‚ÄĒ |
| OpenClaw | http://empirebox.local:7878 | admin / (see .env) |
| API Gateway | http://empirebox.local:8000 | Bearer token in .env |
| Control Center | http://empirebox.local:8001 | API key in .env |

# Quick Start ‚ÄĒ EmpireBox Founders Unit

## TL;DR

```bash
# 1. Create USB (on your laptop)
# Install Ventoy on USB ‚Üí copy Ubuntu 24.04 ISO + this folder

# 2. Boot Beelink EQR5 from USB (F7 boot menu)
# Ubuntu installs automatically ‚ÄĒ wait ~15 min

# 3. First boot runs install script automatically ‚ÄĒ wait ~30 min

# 4. Access your control center
open http://empirebox.local
```

## After Install ‚ÄĒ Key Commands

```bash
# SSH into the box
ssh empirebox@empirebox.local

# List all products
ebox list

# Start a bundle
ebox bundle full          # All 13 products
ebox bundle reseller      # MarketForge + ShipForge + RelistApp + ForgeCRM
ebox bundle contractor    # ContractorForge + LuxeForge + LeadForge + ForgeCRM
ebox bundle support       # SupportForge + ForgeCRM + EmpireAssist

# Start/stop individual products
ebox start marketforge
ebox stop marketforge
ebox logs marketforge

# Check what's running
ebox status
```

## Key URLs

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| Dashboard | http://empirebox.local |
| OpenClaw AI | http://empirebox.local:7878 |
| Control Center | http://empirebox.local:8001 |
| Portainer | http://empirebox.local:9000 |
| Ollama | http://empirebox.local:11434 |

## Credentials

```bash
cat /opt/empirebox/.env
```

# EmpireBox Security Guide

## Founders Unit vs Customer Units

### Founders Unit (This Device)
- **Full access** ‚ÄĒ no guardrails, no sandbox
- **OpenClaw mode**: `founders` ‚ÄĒ can execute any system command
- **No phone-home** ‚ÄĒ not monitored by Control Center
- **Purpose**: Internal management, product development, fleet control

### Customer Units
- **Sandboxed** ‚ÄĒ restricted command execution
- **Phone-home**: POST to `/heartbeat` every 5 minutes
- **License validation** required on startup
- **Remote control**: Can be suspended or revoked from Control Center
- **OpenClaw mode**: `customer` ‚ÄĒ limited to product-specific skills only

## Credential Storage

All credentials are in `/opt/empirebox/.env`:
```
chmod 600 /opt/empirebox/.env
# Owner: root only
```

### What's in .env
- `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` ‚ÄĒ PostgreSQL database password
- `REDIS_PASSWORD` ‚ÄĒ Redis auth password
- `JWT_SECRET` ‚ÄĒ JWT signing secret for API auth
- `CONTROL_API_KEY` ‚ÄĒ Control Center API authentication
- `OPENCLAW_SECRET` ‚ÄĒ OpenClaw admin secret

All passwords are generated with `openssl rand -hex 32` (256-bit entropy).

## Firewall Configuration

UFW rules applied during installation:

| Port | Service | Rule |
|------|---------|------|
| 22 | SSH | ALLOW |
| 80 | Dashboard | ALLOW |
| 443 | HTTPS | ALLOW |
| 7878 | OpenClaw | ALLOW |
| 8000 | API Gateway | ALLOW |
| 8001 | Control Center | ALLOW |
| 8010-8130 | Products | ALLOW |
| 9000 | Portainer | ALLOW |
| 11434 | Ollama | ALLOW |
| 5432 | PostgreSQL | DENY (internal only) |
| 6379 | Redis | DENY (internal only) |

Note: PostgreSQL and Redis are only accessible within the `empirebox` Docker network, not exposed externally.

## Phone-Home System (Customer Units)

Customer units send a heartbeat every 5 minutes:
```http
POST /heartbeat
{
  "unit_id": "UNIT-ABC123",
  "license_key": "EMP-XXXXXXXX-YYYYYYYY",
  "hostname": "customer-unit-1",
  "ip": "192.168.1.100",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uptime_seconds": 86400
}
```

Response tells the unit whether to continue or stop:
```json
{
  "status": "active",
  "action": "continue",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00"
}
```

If `action` is `"stop"`, the customer unit shuts down all products.

## Recommendations

1. **Change SSH password** after first login:
   ```bash
   passwd empirebox
   ```

2. **Rotate credentials** periodically:
   ```bash
   # Edit /opt/empirebox/.env with new passwords
   # Restart services: docker compose restart
   ```

3. **Keep Portainer behind firewall** ‚ÄĒ don't expose port 9000 to the internet.

4. **Use HTTPS** for production deployments ‚ÄĒ add nginx SSL termination.

5. **Backup .env securely** ‚ÄĒ store a copy in a password manager.

# Build all EmpireBox services
docker compose build

#cloud-config
autoinstall:
  version: 1
  locale: en_US.UTF-8
  keyboard:
    layout: us
  identity:
    hostname: empirebox-founders
    username: empirebox
    password: "$6$rounds=4096$empirebox$REPLACE_WITH_HASH_FROM_mkpasswd_-m_sha-512"
    # Generate hash: mkpasswd -m sha-512 founders2026
    realname: EmpireBox
  ssh:
    install-server: true
    allow-pw: true
  packages:
    - curl
    - wget
    - git
    - jq
    - htop
    - vim
    - net-tools
    - avahi-daemon
    - ufw
    - openssh-server
  storage:
    layout:
      name: direct
  late-commands:
    - mkdir -p /target/opt/empirebox
    - "cp -r /cdrom/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/. /target/opt/empirebox/"
    - "chmod +x /target/opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh"
    - "chmod +x /target/opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox"
    - "chmod +x /target/opt/empirebox/scripts/backup.sh"
    - |
      cat > /target/etc/systemd/system/empirebox-install.service << 'EOF'
      [Unit]
      Description=EmpireBox Founders Install
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target
      ConditionPathExists=/opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh

      [Service]
      Type=oneshot
      ExecStart=/opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
      ExecStartPost=/bin/rm -f /etc/systemd/system/empirebox-install.service
      RemainAfterExit=yes
      StandardOutput=journal+console
      StandardError=journal+console

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
      EOF
    - curtin in-target --target=/target systemctl enable empirebox-install.service
  user-data:
    disable_root: false
  updates: security
  shutdown: reboot

              ‚Ēā   License validation          ‚Ēā
              ‚Ēā   Remote suspend/revoke       ‚Ēā
              ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ‚Ēė
```

## Component Descriptions

### Dashboard (Port 80)
nginx serving static HTML. Shows all service links, product grid, and quick command reference.

### OpenClaw (Port 7878)

EOF

# EmpireBox Founders Unit - USB Installer

> ‚ö†ÔłŹ **INTERNAL USE ONLY ‚ÄĒ MASTER CONTROL DEVICE**

This USB installer sets up the EmpireBox Founders Unit on a **Beelink EQR5** (Ryzen 7 5825U, 32GB RAM, 500GB NVMe). This is the MASTER CONTROL device ‚ÄĒ not for sale.

## What Gets Installed

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Ubuntu Server 24.04 | Base OS (unattended install) |
| Docker + Compose | Container runtime |
| Ollama | Local AI (llama3.1:8b, codellama:7b, nomic-embed-text) |
| OpenClaw | AI Agent with multi-model support (founders mode) |
| EmpireBox Core | PostgreSQL, Redis, API Gateway |
| Control Center | Fleet management for customer units |
| All 13 Products | On-demand startup via `ebox` CLI |
| Founders Dashboard | Web UI at port 80 |

## Hardware Requirements

- **Device**: Beelink EQR5
- **CPU**: AMD Ryzen 7 5825U
- **RAM**: 32GB DDR4
- **Storage**: 500GB NVMe SSD
- **USB**: 16GB+ USB drive

## USB Creation (Ventoy)

1. Download [Ventoy](https://www.ventoy.net) and install it to your USB drive
2. Download Ubuntu Server 24.04 ISO
3. **Generate the password hash** (required before burning):
   ```bash
   mkpasswd -m sha-512 founders2026
   # Replace the placeholder in autoinstall/user-data line 10 with the output
   ```
4. Copy the ISO to the Ventoy USB drive
5. Copy this entire `founders_usb_installer/` directory to the USB drive
6. Boot the Beelink EQR5 from USB (press F7 for boot menu)
7. Select the Ubuntu ISO from Ventoy
8. When prompted for autoinstall, Ventoy will use `autoinstall/user-data`

## Installation Steps

1. **Boot from USB** ‚ÄĒ Beelink EQR5 ‚Üí F7 ‚Üí Select Ubuntu ISO
2. **Unattended Install** ‚ÄĒ Ubuntu installs automatically (~10-15 min)
3. **First Boot** ‚ÄĒ System runs `install-founders.sh` automatically (~20-30 min)
4. **Done** ‚ÄĒ Access dashboard at `http://empirebox.local`

## Post-Install Checklist

- [ ] Access dashboard: `http://empirebox.local` or `http://<ip>`
- [ ] Log in to OpenClaw: `http://empirebox.local:7878`
- [ ] Check Control Center: `http://empirebox.local:8001`
- [ ] Add AI API keys in OpenClaw UI (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
- [ ] Test `ebox list` in terminal
- [ ] Run `ebox bundle full` to start all products

## Access URLs

| Service | URL | Credentials |
|---------|-----|-------------|
| Founders Dashboard | http://empirebox.local | ‚ÄĒ |
| OpenClaw | http://empirebox.local:7878 | admin / (see .env) |
| API Gateway | http://empirebox.local:8000 | Bearer token in .env |
| Control Center | http://empirebox.local:8001 | API key in .env |

# Quick Start ‚ÄĒ EmpireBox Founders Unit

## TL;DR

```bash
# 1. Create USB (on your laptop)
# Install Ventoy on USB ‚Üí copy Ubuntu 24.04 ISO + this folder

# 2. Boot Beelink EQR5 from USB (F7 boot menu)
# Ubuntu installs automatically ‚ÄĒ wait ~15 min

# 3. First boot runs install script automatically ‚ÄĒ wait ~30 min

# 4. Access your control center
open http://empirebox.local
```

## After Install ‚ÄĒ Key Commands

```bash
# SSH into the box
ssh empirebox@empirebox.local

# List all products
ebox list

# Start a bundle
ebox bundle full          # All 13 products
ebox bundle reseller      # MarketForge + ShipForge + RelistApp + ForgeCRM
ebox bundle contractor    # ContractorForge + LuxeForge + LeadForge + ForgeCRM
ebox bundle support       # SupportForge + ForgeCRM + EmpireAssist

# Start/stop individual products
ebox start marketforge
ebox stop marketforge
ebox logs marketforge

# Check what's running
ebox status
```

## Key URLs

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| Dashboard | http://empirebox.local |
| OpenClaw AI | http://empirebox.local:7878 |
| Control Center | http://empirebox.local:8001 |
| Portainer | http://empirebox.local:9000 |
| Ollama | http://empirebox.local:11434 |

## Credentials

```bash
cat /opt/empirebox/.env
```

# EmpireBox Security Guide

## Founders Unit vs Customer Units

### Founders Unit (This Device)
- **Full access** ‚ÄĒ no guardrails, no sandbox
- **OpenClaw mode**: `founders` ‚ÄĒ can execute any system command
- **No phone-home** ‚ÄĒ not monitored by Control Center
- **Purpose**: Internal management, product development, fleet control

### Customer Units
- **Sandboxed** ‚ÄĒ restricted command execution
- **Phone-home**: POST to `/heartbeat` every 5 minutes
- **License validation** required on startup
- **Remote control**: Can be suspended or revoked from Control Center
- **OpenClaw mode**: `customer` ‚ÄĒ limited to product-specific skills only

## Credential Storage

All credentials are in `/opt/empirebox/.env`:
```
chmod 600 /opt/empirebox/.env
# Owner: root only
```

### What's in .env
- `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` ‚ÄĒ PostgreSQL database password
- `REDIS_PASSWORD` ‚ÄĒ Redis auth password
- `JWT_SECRET` ‚ÄĒ JWT signing secret for API auth
- `CONTROL_API_KEY` ‚ÄĒ Control Center API authentication
- `OPENCLAW_SECRET` ‚ÄĒ OpenClaw admin secret

All passwords are generated with `openssl rand -hex 32` (256-bit entropy).

## Firewall Configuration

UFW rules applied during installation:

| Port | Service | Rule |
|------|---------|------|
| 22 | SSH | ALLOW |
| 80 | Dashboard | ALLOW |
| 443 | HTTPS | ALLOW |
| 7878 | OpenClaw | ALLOW |
| 8000 | API Gateway | ALLOW |
| 8001 | Control Center | ALLOW |
| 8010-8130 | Products | ALLOW |
| 9000 | Portainer | ALLOW |
| 11434 | Ollama | ALLOW |
| 5432 | PostgreSQL | DENY (internal only) |
| 6379 | Redis | DENY (internal only) |

Note: PostgreSQL and Redis are only accessible within the `empirebox` Docker network, not exposed externally.

## Phone-Home System (Customer Units)

Customer units send a heartbeat every 5 minutes:
```http
POST /heartbeat
{
  "unit_id": "UNIT-ABC123",
  "license_key": "EMP-XXXXXXXX-YYYYYYYY",
  "hostname": "customer-unit-1",
  "ip": "192.168.1.100",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uptime_seconds": 86400
}
```

Response tells the unit whether to continue or stop:
```json
{
  "status": "active",
  "action": "continue",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00"
}
```

If `action` is `"stop"`, the customer unit shuts down all products.

## Recommendations

1. **Change SSH password** after first login:
   ```bash
   passwd empirebox
   ```

2. **Rotate credentials** periodically:
   ```bash
   # Edit /opt/empirebox/.env with new passwords
   # Restart services: docker compose restart
   ```

3. **Keep Portainer behind firewall** ‚ÄĒ don't expose port 9000 to the internet.

4. **Use HTTPS** for production deployments ‚ÄĒ add nginx SSL termination.

5. **Backup .env securely** ‚ÄĒ store a copy in a password manager.

# Build all EmpireBox services
docker compose build

#cloud-config
autoinstall:
  version: 1
  locale: en_US.UTF-8
  keyboard:
    layout: us
  identity:
    hostname: empirebox-founders
    username: empirebox
    password: "$6$rounds=4096$empirebox$REPLACE_WITH_HASH_FROM_mkpasswd_-m_sha-512"
    # Generate hash: mkpasswd -m sha-512 founders2026
    realname: EmpireBox
  ssh:
    install-server: true
    allow-pw: true
  packages:
    - curl
    - wget
    - git
    - jq
    - htop
    - vim
    - net-tools
    - avahi-daemon
    - ufw
    - openssh-server
  storage:
    layout:
      name: direct
  late-commands:
    - mkdir -p /target/opt/empirebox
    - "cp -r /cdrom/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/. /target/opt/empirebox/"
    - "chmod +x /target/opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh"
    - "chmod +x /target/opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox"
    - "chmod +x /target/opt/empirebox/scripts/backup.sh"
    - |
      cat > /target/etc/systemd/system/empirebox-install.service << 'EOF'
      [Unit]
      Description=EmpireBox Founders Install
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target
      ConditionPathExists=/opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh

      [Service]
      Type=oneshot
      ExecStart=/opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
      ExecStartPost=/bin/rm -f /etc/systemd/system/empirebox-install.service
      RemainAfterExit=yes
      StandardOutput=journal+console
      StandardError=journal+console

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
      EOF
    - curtin in-target --target=/target systemctl enable empirebox-install.service
  user-data:
    disable_root: false
  updates: security
  shutdown: reboot

## Component Descriptions

### Dashboard (Port 80)

### OpenClaw (Port 7878)

#!/bin/bash
# EmpireBox Product Launcher CLI
# Usage: ebox <command> [args]

INSTALL_PATH="/opt/empirebox"
PRODUCTS_PATH="${INSTALL_PATH}/products"
ENV_FILE="${INSTALL_PATH}/.env"

# Load env if present
[ -f "${ENV_FILE}" ] && set -a && source "${ENV_FILE}" && set +a

# Product port map
declare -A PRODUCT_PORTS=(
  [marketforge]=8010
  [contractorforge]=8020
  [luxeforge]=8030
  [supportforge]=8040
  [leadforge]=8050
  [shipforge]=8060
  [forgecrm]=8070
  [relistapp]=8080
  [socialforge]=8090
  [llcfactory]=8100
  [apostapp]=8110
  [empireassist]=8120
  [empirepay]=8130
)

# Bundle definitions
declare -A BUNDLES=(
  [reseller]="marketforge shipforge relistapp forgecrm"
  [contractor]="contractorforge luxeforge leadforge forgecrm"
  [support]="supportforge forgecrm empireassist"
  [full]="marketforge contractorforge luxeforge supportforge leadforge shipforge forgecrm relistapp socialforge llcfactory apostapp empireassist empirepay"
)

PRODUCTS=("marketforge" "contractorforge" "luxeforge" "supportforge" "leadforge"
          "shipforge" "forgecrm" "relistapp" "socialforge" "llcfactory"
          "apostapp" "empireassist" "empirepay")

TELEGRAM_PATH="${INSTALL_PATH}/telegram"
VOICE_PATH="${INSTALL_PATH}/voice"

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
EmpireBox Product Launcher

COMMANDS:
  ebox list                  List all products with ports and status
  ebox status                Show running products
  ebox start <product>       Start a product
  ebox stop <product>        Stop a product
  ebox restart <product>     Restart a product
  ebox logs <product>        View product logs
  ebox bundle <name>         Start a bundle (reseller|contractor|support|full)
  ebox stop-all              Stop all products
  ebox health                System health check (core + voice + products)
  ebox telegram start        Start the Telegram bot
  ebox telegram stop         Stop the Telegram bot
  ebox telegram logs         View Telegram bot logs
  ebox telegram status       Show Telegram bot status
  ebox voice start           Start the voice service
  ebox voice stop            Stop the voice service
  ebox voice logs            View voice service logs
  ebox voice status          Show voice service status

PRODUCTS:
EOF
  for p in "${PRODUCTS[@]}"; do
    printf "  %-18s port %s\n" "$p" "${PRODUCT_PORTS[$p]}"
  done
  cat <<EOF

BUNDLES:
  reseller    marketforge, shipforge, relistapp, forgecrm
  contractor  contractorforge, luxeforge, leadforge, forgecrm
  support     supportforge, forgecrm, empireassist
  full        all 13 products
EOF
}

is_running() {
  local product="$1"
  docker compose -f "${PRODUCTS_PATH}/${product}/docker-compose.yml" \
    ps --services --filter "status=running" 2>/dev/null \

FROM python:3.12-slim

COPY . .

ENV CONFIG_PATH=/app/config.yaml

CMD ["python", "bot.py"]

messages.content: we need to rinstall ubunto on mini pc, something is off nad it has crashed 5 times. I just notice that after installing and runnig OS in the unit, reboot kept USB as first bootable devide, I forgot to change it, could this be the problem?, I am running this chat from try install still on USB flash drive. should I do full reinstall?

Yes, **that's very likely the problem!** Here's what's happening:

## ūüĒć The Issue

When you boot from USB with "Try Ubuntu" or the installer still active:
- The system is running **entirely from RAM + USB**
- Any writes may be going to a temporary filesystem
- The actual NVMe install isn't being used
- This causes instability and crashes

**The USB being first in boot order means:**
- Every reboot goes back to the USB installer instead of the NVMe
- The actual installed system on NVMe never runs
- The `install-founders.sh` script may have never executed properly

---

## ‚úÖ Recommended: Full Reinstall (Clean Slate)

Yes, do a **full reinstall**. It's the cleanest path forward:

### Step 1: Fix Boot Order FIRST
1. **Shut down** the Beelink completely
2. **Remove the USB drive**
3. **Power on** and press **DEL** or **F2** to enter BIOS/UEFI
4. Go to **Boot** settings
5. Set **NVMe/Internal SSD as first boot device**
6. **Save & Exit**

### Step 2: Check Current State
With USB removed, boot the system:
- **If it boots to Ubuntu** ‚Üí The OS installed but `install-founders.sh` may not have run
- **If it doesn't boot** ‚Üí Need full reinstall

### Step 3: Full Reinstall (if needed)

1. **Re-insert USB**
2. **Boot from USB** (F7 boot menu)
3. **Select Ubuntu ISO** in Ventoy
4. Let the **unattended autoinstall** run (~10-15 min)
5. **IMPORTANT:** When it reboots, **remove the USB immediately** or enter BIOS and change boot order
6. Let first boot run `install-founders.sh` (~20-30 min)

---

## ūüõ†ÔłŹ Quick Fix If OS Is Already Installed

If the NVMe has Ubuntu installed and just needs the EmpireBox setup:

```bash
# Boot without USB, then SSH in or use local terminal
ssh empirebox@empirebox.local
# or login locally with: empirebox / founders2026

# Check if install script exists
ls -la /opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh

# If it exists but never ran, run it manually:
sudo /opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
```

---

## ūüďč What To Do Right Now

1. **Shut down** the system (don't just reboot)
2. **Remove the USB drive**
3. **Enter BIOS** (DEL or F2 on boot)
4. **Change boot order** ‚Üí NVMe first
5. **Save & boot**

Then tell me:
- Does it boot to Ubuntu?
- Or does it fail to boot?

Based on that, we'll either run the install script manually or do a clean reinstall.

**What do you want to do?**
it booted in ubunto now

Great! The OS is installed. Now let's check if the EmpireBox setup ran or needs to be run manually.

## ūüĒć Check Current State

Run these commands:

```bash
# Check if you're logged in as empirebox user
whoami

# Check if the install script exists
ls -la /opt/empirebox/

# Check if Docker is installed
docker --version

# Check if the ebox CLI exists
which ebox
```

**Tell me what you see** - this will tell us:
1. If `/opt/empirebox/` exists and has files
2. If Docker is installed
3. If the setup script ran or not

---

## Likely Scenarios

### Scenario A: `/opt/empirebox/` exists but setup didn't run
```bash
# Run the install script manually
sudo /opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
```
This will take ~20-30 min to install Docker, pull Ollama models, set up everything.

### Scenario B: `/opt/empirebox/` is empty or missing
The autoinstall didn't copy files properly - we'll need to pull from GitHub and run setup.

### Scenario C: Everything is already installed
```bash
# Check status
ebox status
ebox health
```

---

**Run those diagnostic commands and paste the output - I'll tell you exactly what to do next!**
signed in to portainer

Excellent! **Portainer is running** ‚ÄĒ that means the install script ran successfully! ūüéČ

## ‚úÖ System is Installed!

If Portainer is up at `:9000`, the core infrastructure should be working.

## ūüĒć Let's Verify Everything

SSH into the box (or use the terminal) and run:

```bash
# Check all services
ebox status

# List all products
ebox list

# System health check
ebox health
```

## ūüĆź Check These URLs

| Service | URL | Status |
|---------|-----|--------|
| **Dashboard** | http://empirebox.local | Try it |
| **OpenClaw AI** | http://empirebox.local:7878 | Try it |
| **Control Center** | http://empirebox.local:8001 | Try it |
| **Portainer** | http://empirebox.local:9000 | ‚úÖ Working |
| **Ollama** | http://empirebox.local:11434 | Try it |

## ūüöÄ Start the Products

If core is running, start your products:

```bash
# Start ALL 13 products
ebox bundle full

# Or check what's already running
docker ps
```

---

**What do you see in Portainer?** 
- How many containers are running?
- Any containers in error/stopped state?

And can you access the other URLs (Dashboard, OpenClaw, etc.)?

rg@EmpireBox:~$ # Check if you're logged in as empirebox user
whoami

# Check if the install script exists
ls -la /opt/empirebox/

# Check if Docker is installed
docker --version

# Check if the ebox CLI exists
which ebox
rg
ls: cannot access '/opt/empirebox/': No such file or directory
Docker version 28.2.2, build 28.2.2-0ubuntu1~24.04.1
rg@EmpireBox:~$

Ah, I see the issue! The system has **Docker installed** (from a previous attempt or manual install), but the **EmpireBox files were never copied** from the USB during autoinstall.

## ūüĒć Current State
- ‚úÖ Ubuntu is installed
- ‚úÖ Docker is installed
- ‚ĚĆ `/opt/empirebox/` doesn't exist
- ‚ĚĆ EmpireBox setup never ran
- ‚ö†ÔłŹ You're logged in as `rg`, not `empirebox`

## ūüõ†ÔłŹ Let's Fix This

We need to pull the EmpireBox files from GitHub and run the setup:

```bash
# Create the directory
sudo mkdir -p /opt/empirebox

# Clone the repo (or just the founders installer)
cd /tmp
git clone https://github.com/r22gir/Empire.git

# Copy the founders installer files
sudo cp -r /tmp/Empire/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/* /opt/empirebox/

# Make scripts executable
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/scripts/backup.sh

# Check files are there
ls -la /opt/empirebox/
```

Then run the install script:

```bash
# Run the master install
sudo /opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
```

This will take **~20-30 minutes** to:
- Set up PostgreSQL, Redis
- Install Ollama and download AI models (llama3.1:8b, codellama:7b, nomic-embed-text)
- Configure OpenClaw, Control Center
- Set up all 13 products
- Configure firewall
- Create the `ebox` CLI symlink

---

**Run those commands and let me know when the install script starts (or if you hit any errors)!**

rg@EmpireBox:~$ # Create the directory
sudo mkdir -p /opt/empirebox

# Clone the repo (or just the founders installer)
cd /tmp
git clone https://github.com/r22gir/Empire.git

# Copy the founders installer files
sudo cp -r /tmp/Empire/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/* /opt/empirebox/

# Make scripts executable
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/install-founders.sh
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/scripts/backup.sh

# Check files are there
ls -la /opt/empirebox/

The repo is **private**, so we need to authenticate. Let's use a GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) instead of password.

## Option 1: Use GitHub Token (Recommended)

### Step 1: Generate a Personal Access Token
1. Go to: https://github.com/settings/tokens
2. Click **"Generate new token (classic)"**
3. Give it a name like "EmpireBox Install"
4. Select scopes: **`repo`** (full control of private repos)
5. Click **Generate token**
6. **Copy the token** (you won't see it again!)

### Step 2: Clone with Token
```bash
cd /tmp
git clone https://r22gir:<YOUR_TOKEN_HERE>@github.com/r22gir/Empire.git
```

Replace `<YOUR_TOKEN_HERE>` with your actual token.

---

## Option 2: Download as ZIP from GitHub

If you're already logged into GitHub on another device:

1. Go to https://github.com/r22gir/Empire
2. Click **Code** ‚Üí **Download ZIP**
3. Transfer the ZIP to the Beelink via USB or `scp`
4. Then:
```bash
cd /tmp
unzip Empire-main.zip
sudo cp -r /tmp/Empire-main/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/* /opt/empirebox/
```

---

## Option 3: Copy from USB (if you still have it)

If your USB drive has the `founders_usb_installer/` folder:

```bash
# Mount USB (find it first)
lsblk

# Usually it's /dev/sda1 or similar
sudo mount /dev/sda1 /mnt

# Copy files
sudo cp -r /mnt/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/* /opt/empirebox/

# Unmount
sudo umount /mnt
```

---

**Which option works best for you?** 
- Token auth
- ZIP download
- Copy from USB

## ūüĒß Set Up the `ebox` CLI

The only thing missing is the `ebox` CLI shortcut. Let's fix that:

```bash
# Copy files from the cloned repo (if clone finished)

# Make ebox executable and link it
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox
sudo ln -sf /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox /usr/local/bin/ebox

# Test it
ebox list
ebox status
```

Yeah, 55 KiB/s is painfully slow! ūüźĆ

**Options:**

### Option 1: Wait it out (~5-10 min at this speed)
It's 71% done, maybe 3-5 min left.

### Option 2: Cancel and do a shallow clone (faster)
```bash
# Press Ctrl+C to cancel

# Then do a shallow clone (only latest commit, no history)
cd /tmp
rm -rf Empire 2>/dev/null
git clone --depth 1 https://r22gir:<YOUR_TOKEN>@github.com/r22gir/Empire.git
```

This skips all the git history and just gets the current files - **much faster!**

### Option 3: Just download the specific folder we need

Since EmpireBox is already running and we just need the `ebox` CLI script, we can create it manually:

```bash
# Cancel the clone (Ctrl+C)

sudo curl -o /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox https://raw.githubusercontent.com/r22gir/Empire/main/founders_usb_installer/empirebox/scripts/ebox
sudo chmod +x /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox
sudo ln -sf /opt/empirebox/scripts/ebox /usr/local/bin/ebox

# Test
ebox list
```

The warnings are minor (just version deprecation notices). But we need to **create the `.env` file**. Let's do that:

```bash
# Create the .env file with secure passwords
sudo bash -c 'cat > /opt/empirebox/.env << EOF
# EmpireBox Environment Configuration
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
REDIS_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
CONTROL_API_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
OPENCLAW_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)

# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@localhost:5432/empirebox

# Mode
EMPIREBOX_MODE=founders
EOF'

# Secure the file
sudo chmod 600 /opt/empirebox/.env

# Check it was created
cat /opt/empirebox/.env
```

Then check if products are running:

```bash
# Give it a moment to start, then check
sleep 10
ebox list
docker ps
```

Two issues to fix:

1. **`.env` file permissions** - ebox can't read it
2. **Products didn't actually start** - they show stopped

Let's fix both:

```bash
# Fix .env permissions (readable by all, but only writable by root)
sudo chmod 644 /opt/empirebox/.env

# Check what happened with products - look at logs
docker ps -a | head -20

# Try starting one product manually to see the error
cd /opt/empirebox/products/marketforge
sudo docker compose up -d
```

The product Docker images don't exist on Docker Hub - they need to be **built locally**. Let's build and start marketforge:

```bash
# Check if there's a Dockerfile
ls -la /opt/empirebox/products/marketforge/

# Build the image locally
cd /opt/empirebox/products/marketforge
sudo docker compose build

# Then start it
sudo docker compose up -d
```

This will build the image from source. Let's see what's in that directory first!

The backend code with Dockerfiles is in `/tmp/Empire/backend/`. We need to build from there. Let's do it:

```bash
# Build marketforge from the backend source
cd /tmp/Empire/backend
sudo docker build -t empirebox/marketforge:latest .

# Now start it
cd /opt/empirebox/products/marketforge
sudo docker compose up -d

# Check if it's running
docker ps | grep marketforge
```

This will build the actual product from source code! If this works, we'll need to do the same for the other products.
